Nate McGraw's Security Blog

Cybersecurity Writeups by Nate McGraw

Home About Me Github LinkedIn

Hi, I’m Nate McGraw

I’m a cybersecurity professional actively pursuing a penetration testing or red team role, with a strong focus on real-world attacker tradecraft, Active Directory exploitation, and adversary emulation.

I hold the Offensive Security Certified Professional (OSCP) and a Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity and Information Assurance from Western Governors University. I’m currently preparing for the Certified Red Team Operator (CRTO) exam, with a focus on advanced Active Directory tradecraft, Kerberos abuse, and post-exploitation techniques.

My background spans SOC operations, vulnerability research, and hands-on offensive security across Windows, Linux, cloud, and Active Directory environments.

What I Focus On

My primary interests and strengths are in offensive security and red team operations, including:

  • Active Directory attacks (Kerberos, ADCS, trusts, delegation, lateral movement)
  • Internal network compromise and multi-hop pivoting
  • Privilege escalation on Windows and Linux
  • Credential access and abuse (hashes, tickets, certificates)
  • Adversary emulation aligned with real-world attack paths

I spend a significant amount of time in labs and CTFs, deliberately chaining vulnerabilities together rather than treating issues in isolation. This site documents those attack paths and the reasoning behind them.

Professional Experience

Most recently, I worked as a Cybersecurity Analyst at Zyston, where I monitored and investigated security events for 50+ client environments across multiple industries. On a typical day, I handled dozens of investigations involving SIEM, EDR, and cloud telemetry, with a strong emphasis on determining true positives and escalating meaningful incidents.

That experience sharpened my ability to:

  • Identify attacker behavior quickly
  • Understand how detection gaps are created
  • Evaluate exploitability and impact from both offensive and defensive perspectives

Earlier in my career, I worked as a full-stack developer with a front-end focus, primarily building modern React.js applications backed by secure APIs. My work emphasized:

  • Designing and implementing secure React front-ends
  • Consuming and integrating authenticated APIs
  • Implementing client-side input validation and secure data handling
  • Working closely with backend services (Ruby on Rails) to enforce authentication, authorization, and business logic

This development background informs my offensive security work by giving me a strong understanding of how real applications are built — and how they are commonly misconfigured or exposed.

Before that, I supported large government environments as a Help Desk Analyst, managing identity, access controls, and user permissions at scale.

Offensive Security & Research

I maintain a hands-on offensive skillset and have disclosed multiple vulnerabilities publicly, including CVE-2024-42849 and CVE-2024-42850.

Certifications and training that best reflect my current focus include:

  • Offensive Security Certified Professional (OSCP)
  • Certified Red Team Operator (CRTO) — in progress
  • Certified AppSec Practitioner (CAP)
  • CompTIA Security+, CySA+, Network+
  • Linux Essentials

I prioritize practical exploitation and understanding over checklists, and I continuously refine my methodology through labs, tooling, and self-directed research.

This Site

This site serves as a technical portfolio and learning journal. You’ll find:

  • Detailed CTF and lab write-ups focused on realistic attack paths
  • Notes on Active Directory exploitation and post-exploitation tradecraft
  • Tooling experiments, environment setup, and tunneling techniques
  • Reflections on offensive security learning and methodology

Everything here is written to demonstrate how I think as an operator, not just what tools I can run.

Open to Opportunities

I am actively seeking opportunities in penetration testing, red team, or adversary emulation roles where I can continue developing as an operator and contribute meaningful findings.

  • LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nate-mcgraw-521930176/
  • GitHub: https://github.com/njmbb8

If you’re hiring for offensive security roles or want to discuss tradecraft, I’m always open to a conversation.
